cases.question: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.reference: There are no material differences between handbook_alpha and handbook_eta. Both allow remote work 2 days per week with manager approval and require a security checklist before each remote day, even though handbook_eta paraphrases the wording.
cases.normalizedReference: There are no material differences between handbook_alpha and handbook_eta. Both allow remote work 2 days per week with manager approval and require a security checklist before each remote day, even though handbook_eta paraphrases the wording.
cases.rawResponse:
Summary:
- Both documents allow up to 2 days of remote work per week with manager approval and require completing a security checklist before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].

Per document:
- handbook-alpha.pdf:
  - May work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval [Source 1].
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day [Source 1].
- handbook-eta.pdf:
  - May work from home up to 2 days each week when a manager approves the arrangement [Source 2].
  - Complete the security checklist before every remote day [Source 2].

Agreements:
- Limit of 2 remote days per week [Source 1][Source 2].
- Manager approval is required [Source 1][Source 2].
- Security checklist must be completed before each remote day [Source 1][Source 2].

Differences:
- No substantive policy differences are evident in the provided excerpts; wording varies slightly (“work remotely” vs. “work from home”) but requirements match [Source 1][Source 2].

Gaps or uncertainty:
- The excerpts do not specify eligibility criteria, equipment/support, reimbursement, working hours/availability expectations, or processes for exceptions or fully remote arrangements.
cases.normalizedResponse: Both documents allow up to 2 days of remote work per week with manager approval and require completing a security checklist before each remote day . Limit of 2 remote days per week . Manager approval is required . Security checklist must be completed before each remote day . No substantive policy differences are evident in the provided excerpts; wording varies slightly (“work remotely” vs. “work from home”) but requirements match .

compareJudge.verdict: The candidate answer effectively summarizes the main agreements and acknowledges slight wording variations.
strengths: Accurately captures main agreements between documents

cases.question: Compare the remote work policy in these documents.
cases.reference: Both documents allow remote work 2 days per week with manager approval and require security checklists, but handbook_delta adds an eligibility restriction limiting the policy to full-time engineering employees.
cases.normalizedReference: Both documents allow remote work 2 days per week with manager approval and require security checklists, but handbook_delta adds an eligibility restriction limiting the policy to full-time engineering employees.
cases.rawResponse:
Summary:
- Both policies allow remote work up to 2 days per week with manager approval and require completion of security checklists before each remote day. [Source 1][Source 2]
- The delta handbook limits eligibility to full-time engineering employees, while the alpha handbook does not state such a limitation. [Source 2][Source 1]

Per document:
- handbook-alpha.pdf:
  - Employees may work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval. [Source 1]
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day. [Source 1]
- handbook-delta.pdf:
  - Only full-time engineering employees may work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval. [Source 2]
  - Security checklists must be completed before each remote day. [Source 2]

Agreements:
- Remote work is limited to 2 days per week with manager approval in both documents. [Source 1][Source 2]
- Security checklists are required before each remote day in both documents. [Source 1][Source 2]

Differences:
- Eligibility scope: Delta restricts remote work to full-time engineering employees; Alpha does not specify any department or employment-status restriction in the provided excerpt. [Source 2][Source 1]

Gaps or uncertainty:
- The alpha excerpt does not indicate whether other sections impose role- or status-based limits beyond “Employees.” [Source 1]
- The approval process details and the contents/requirements of the security checklists are not described in either excerpt. [Source 1][Source 2]
cases.normalizedResponse: Both policies allow remote work up to 2 days per week with manager approval and require completion of security checklists before each remote day. The delta handbook limits eligibility to full-time engineering employees, while the alpha handbook does not state such a limitation. Remote work is limited to 2 days per week with manager approval in both documents. Security checklists are required before each remote day in both documents. Eligibility scope: Delta restricts remote work to full-time engineering employees; Alpha does not specify any department or employment-status restriction in the provided excerpt.

compareJudge.verdict: The candidate answer accurately captures the main agreements and differences between the documents.
strengths: Clearly states agreement on remote work days and security requirements.
Correctly identifies eligibility restriction in delta handbook.
issues: Minor redundancy in stating remote work limitations.
